text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM CONTRAST
(Concurrent Restaging Examination)

CLINICAL INDICATION:
63-year-old female with known Stage IIIB right upper lobe pulmonary adenocarcinoma, EGFR exon 21 L858R-mutant, currently on osi (Tagrisso) 80 mg daily as first-line therapy. Presenting for routine restaging imaging at approximately 14 months of treatment. Patient reports recent onset of worsening exertional dyspnea and new bifrontal headaches over the past 3 to 4 weeks. Dedicated brain MRI obtained concurrently given new neurologic symptoms.

TECHNIQUE:
CT: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis acquired in the portal venous phase following the intravenous administration of 80 mL of nonionic iodinated contrast material (Omnipaque 350) without complication. 3 mm axial reconstructions with coronal and sagittal reformats were generated. Lung windows, mediastinal windows, and soft tissue windows reviewed.

MRI: Multiplanar, multisequence MRI of the brain was performed on a 3.0 Tesla scanner. Sequences include: axial T1 MPRAGE pre- and post-gadolinium, axial T2, axial FLAIR, axial DWI/ADC, and axial susceptibility-weighted imaging (SWI). 7.5 mL of gadolinium-based contrast agent (gadobutrol, Gadavist) administered intravenously without complication.

---

CT CHEST FINDINGS:

Lungs and Airways:
The right upper lobe primary mass, previously measuring 1.8 cm on the 9-month restaging examination, has substantially enlarged. It now measures 3.2 cm in longest axial dimension, representing a marked increase in size compared to the prior nadir. The lesion demonstrates irregular spiculated margins with pleural tethering along the apical aspect of the right upper lobe. There is no significant cavitation. The central lucency previously noted on prior examinations is not well-appreciated on this study. The mass abuts but does not frankly invade the right upper lobe bronchus.

Compared to the prior examination, the degree of enlargement is considerable -- the mass is nearly twice the size it was at nadir, and approaches the dimensions seen at initial presentation (original size 4.8 cm). This growth trajectory is not consistent with ongoing treatment response and raises strong concern for loss of therapeutic efficacy.

In the right lower lobe, no new nodular lesion is identified on this examination. The left lung is clear. No new pulmonary nodules or masses are detected elsewhere. Bilateral lung fields show mild background emphysematous changes consistent with the patient's known smoking history and COPD, similar to prior.

Mediastinum and Hila:
Right paratracheal region (station 4R): A discrete soft tissue nodal density measuring approximately 1.4 cm in short axis is now visible in the right paratracheal space. This node was noted to be subcentimeter and within normal limits on the 9-month restaging examination (previously 0.6 cm). The current nodal enlargement represents reappearance of adenopathy at a site of prior disease. The node has a rounded morphology with loss of fatty hilum, raising concern for recurrent nodal involvement.

Subcarinal region (station 7): A small subcentimeter node measuring 0.5 cm is again seen in the subcarinal space, unchanged from prior. No new subcarinal lymphadenopathy.

Left hilar and mediastinal structures: No pathologically enlarged left-sided mediastinal or hilar nodes. The aortopulmonary window is clear. The prevascular space is unremarkable.

Pleura and Pericardium:
There is a new small right-sided pleural effusion, layering dependently along the right posterior and lateral pleural space. This effusion is simple in attenuation and modest in volume, estimated at approximately 80-100 mL. No left pleural effusion. No pneumothorax. The pericardium is unremarkable with no pericardial effusion.

Chest Wall and Osseous Structures:
No suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesion in the visualized ribs, thoracic vertebrae, or sternum. No pathologic fracture. No definite cortical destruction.

Upper Abdominal Contents (on chest CT scout):
Imaged portions of the upper abdomen are addressed under the abdominal/pelvic section.

---

CT ABDOMEN/PELVIS FINDINGS:

Liver:
The liver is normal in size and demonstrates homogeneous enhancement without focal hepatic lesion. No hypodense or hypervascular mass. No biliary ductal dilatation. The gallbladder is unremarkable.

Spleen:
Unremarkable. No splenomegaly.

Pancreas:
The pancreas is normal in size, contour, and enhancement pattern. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilatation.

Adrenal Glands:
Bilateral adrenal glands are unremarkable in size and configuration. No adrenal mass or nodularity. No definite adrenal metastasis.

Kidneys and Collecting System:
Bilateral kidneys are normal in size with symmetric cortical enhancement. No hydronephrosis. No renal mass identified. Simple-appearing subcentimeter renal cortical cysts bilaterally, unchanged and consistent with benign cysts.

Bowel:
No bowel obstruction. No free air. No mesenteric or peritoneal nodularity or soft tissue stranding to suggest peritoneal disease.

Retroperitoneum:
No retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. Abdominal aorta is normal in caliber.

Pelvis:
No pelvic lymphadenopathy. Uterus is small and likely postmenopausal in appearance. No adnexal mass. Bladder unremarkable.

Osseous Structures (Abdomen/Pelvis):
No suspicious lytic or blastic osseous lesion in the lumbar vertebrae, sacrum, or pelvic bones. No cortical destruction.

---

MRI BRAIN FINDINGS:

Overview:
This is a new study since the baseline MRI brain obtained at initial staging, which was unremarkable. The current examination demonstrates two new enhancing intraparenchymal lesions not previously present.

Right Frontal Lobe:
A well-circumscribed, spherical lesion measuring approximately 1.1 cm in greatest dimension is identified in the right frontal lobe at the gray-white matter junction, in the posterior frontal region. On T1 pre-contrast, the lesion is isointense to mildly hypointense. Following gadolinium administration, there is intense and homogeneous ring-like and nodular enhancement of the lesion. The lesion is surrounded by a modest halo of T2/FLAIR hyperintense signal extending into the adjacent white matter, reflecting vasogenic edema, though the degree of mass effect is modest. No significant midline shift is identified. DWI does not demonstrate restricted diffusion centrally. SWI does not reveal definite internal susceptibility artifact to suggest hemorrhage.

Right Cerebellar Hemisphere:
A second new enhancing lesion is identified in the right cerebellar hemisphere, measuring approximately 0.8 cm. The lesion demonstrates similar homogeneous post-contrast enhancement. Surrounding T2/FLAIR signal abnormality is mild. No mass effect on the fourth ventricle. No obstructive hydrocephalus.

Remainder of Brain Parenchyma:
The cerebral cortex and underlying white matter are otherwise unremarkable. No additional enhancing lesions identified. No evidence of diffuse leptomeningeal enhancement on post-contrast FLAIR or T1 sequences. Basal ganglia, thalami, brainstem, and cerebellum (aside from the noted lesion) appear normal. The ventricles are normal in size and configuration. No midline shift.

Vascular Structures:
No susceptibility artifact to suggest large territorial infarct or hemorrhage beyond what is described. Major intracranial flow voids are intact.

Skull and Calvarium:
No definite calvarial lesion or marrow signal abnormality to suggest osseous metastasis.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. DISEASE PROGRESSION -- RIGHT UPPER LOBE PRIMARY MASS: The right upper lobe pulmonary adenocarcinoma has grown substantially from 1.8 cm at the last restaging to 3.2 cm on this examination. This represents a nearly 80% increase in size from the best imaging response achieved on osi therapy, and is clearly outside the range of interval variation. The mass now approaches but does not yet reach its original pre-treatment size. The growth pattern is strongly suggestive of acquired resistance to osi.

2. NEW RIGHT PARATRACHEAL LYMPHADENOPATHY: Re-emergence of an enlarged right paratracheal (station 4R) lymph node measuring 1.4 cm, which had normalized to subcentimeter dimensions on prior restaging. This is concerning for recurrent regional nodal involvement and is in keeping with progressive systemic disease.

3. NEW SMALL RIGHT PLEURAL EFFUSION: A small new right-sided pleural effusion is present, which may reflect lymphatic obstruction, pleural seeding, or reactive change in the setting of progressive thoracic disease. Clinical correlation warranted.

4. NEW INTRACRANIAL METASTASES: Two new enhancing intraparenchymal lesions are identified -- a 1.1 cm lesion at the right frontal gray-white junction and a 0.8 cm lesion in the right cerebellar hemisphere. The imaging characteristics of both lesions (well-circumscribed, homogeneous ring enhancement, gray-white junction location) are consistent with metastatic deposits in the appropriate clinical context of advanced EGFR-mutant NSCLC. No evidence of leptomeningeal carcinomatosis on this examination.

5. No new intra-abdominal or pelvic metastatic disease. Adrenal glands remain normal. No hepatic metastases.

Correlation with clinical examination and consideration of tissue re-biopsy and/or liquid biopsy for resistance profiling is suggested given the pattern of systemic and intracranial progression after a prolonged period of disease control on Tagrisso.
